retrieval_text: Thyme is a culinary medicinal plant profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 147 works and 249 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Thyme. Common plant-part co-mentions include Flower, Leaf, Resin, Root, Gum, Seed. Common preparation co-mentions include Oil, Powder, Ointment, Essence, Liniment, Tincture. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Rheumatism, Cough, Wounds or ulcers, Colic, Vomiting or nausea. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Toxicity watch, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Bleeding caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; Science papers : chiefly pharmacological and botanical / by Daniel Hanbury; edited, with a memoir, by Joseph Ince.; Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.; Science papers, chiefly pharmacological and botanical / by Daniel Hanbury ... Edited, with memoir by Joseph Ince.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
